             <remarks>
             <para>
             The clone will have the same values for all properties as the original instance. Any collections, like <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.AdditionalProperties"/>,
             are shallow-cloned, meaning a new collection instance is created, but any references contained by the collections are shared with the original.
             </para>
             <para>
             Derived types should override <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions.Clone"/> to return an instance of the derived type.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession">
             <summary>
             Base abstraction for all agent threads.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> contains the state of a specific conversation with an agent which may include:
             <list type="bullet">
             <item><description>Conversation history or a reference to externally stored conversation history.</description></item>
             <item><description>Memories or a reference to externally stored memories.</description></item>
             <item><description>Any other state that the agent needs to persist across runs for a conversation.</description></item>
             </list>
             </para>
             <para>
             An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> may also have behaviors attached to it that may include:
             <list type="bullet">
             <item><description>Customized storage of state.</description></item>
             <item><description>Data extraction from and injection into a conversation.</description></item>
             <item><description>Chat history reduction, e.g. where messages needs to be summarized or truncated to reduce the size.</description></item>
             </list>
             An <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> is always constructed by an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/> so that the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent"/>
             can attach any necessary behaviors to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/>. See the <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.CreateSessionAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
             and <see cref="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent.DeserializeSessionAsync(System.Text.Json.JsonElement,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> methods for more information.
             </para>
             <para>
             Because of these behaviors, an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> may not be reusable across different agents, since each agent
             may add different behaviors to the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession"/> it creates.
             </para>

1094.             <summary>

1097.             <remarks>
1098.             <para>

1100.             A typical response will contain a single message, however a response may contain multiple messages
1101.             in a variety of scenarios. For example, if the agent internally invokes functions or tools, performs
1102.             RAG retrievals or has other complex logic, a single run by the agent may produce many messages showing

toolArgs.prompt:
Explore the Microsoft.Agents.AI library API surface to understand how to create an agent with tool calling. Look at:

1. `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai/1.6.2/lib/net10.0/Microsoft.Agents.AI.xml` - the XML docs
2. `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai.abstractions/1.6.2/lib/net10.0/Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions.xml` - abstractions XML docs

I need to understand:
- How to create a `ChatClientAgent` or equivalent
- How to register tools/functions with the agent
- How to invoke the agent and get responses
- What options are available (MaximumIterations, etc.)
- The key classes and their constructors

Report the exact class names, constructors, and method signatures needed to wire up an agent with tools.

Based on the skill guidance:
- Use Microsoft.Agents.AI for agentic workflows with tool calling
- Use MEAI IChatClient as the abstraction
- Use AIFunction.Create or AITool methods to define tools


- Configure iteration limits, temperature, and other model parameters

Now I'll write the implementation.

data.content:
Everything builds cleanly. Here's what was built:

**ResearchAgent** — a .NET 10 console app using **Microsoft Agent Framework** (`Microsoft.Agents.AI`) for agentic orchestration on top of **MEAI** (`IChatClient`).

### Architecture
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Program.cs` | Wires up DI, creates `ChatClientAgent` with 3 tools, runs the agent loop |
| `WebSearchTool.cs` | Web search — uses **Google Custom Search API** when configured, otherwise returns simulated results |
| `NoteTakingService.cs` | In-memory note storage for saving/retrieving research findings |

### Agent Tools
1. **SearchAsync** — searches the web for a query
2. **SaveNote** — saves a titled note with content
3. **GetNotes** — retrieves all saved notes

### How to run
```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
# Optional for live search:
export GOOGLE_SEARCH_API_KEY="..."
export GOOGLE_SEARCH_ENGINE_ID="..."

dotnet run --project ResearchAgent
```

The agent autonomously decomposes the topic into queries, searches, saves findings as notes, then produces a cited summary — all orchestrated by the Agent Framework's built-in tool dispatch loop.
